sections.content:
Wait, the segmentation only shows 1 small colored square with color 'c' (charcoal) at [22, 34]. But the image clearly shows multiple colored squares in both boxes. This is strange - the segmentation must be grouping things differently.
Let me look at the image more carefully:
- Red box: orange, purple, green_connector, blue
- Green box: red, yellow, magenta, light_green
- Palette: all gray (empty)
But segmentation only found one 4-pixel object (charcoal at 22,34). The colored squares must be larger or grouped differently. Let me check all nodes.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check ALL nodes to understand the current board state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    center = [(b[0][0]+b[2][0])//2, (b[0][1]+b[2][1])//2]
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} center={center} children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
